description:
Testing-review H2. Per-mutator suites feed malformed shapes to validate/1 (asserting {:skip, :structurally_invalid}) but mutate/1 is only ever called on already-validated nodes. mutate/1 runs at ENUMERATION time (mutation_enumerator), OUTSIDE the per-site with_restore/try, so a FunctionClauseError/MatchError on a head-matching-but-degenerate node (e.g. {:|>, meta, [one_arg]}, empty with-clause list, malformed case do:) propagates out of phase_enumerator and aborts the whole 'mix mutagen' run.

Files: test/mutagen_ex/mutators/*_test.exs (pattern across all 11); lib/mutagen_ex/mutators/{pipeline,with_swap,...}.ex.

Fix: add a shared mutate_resilience_test.exs that feeds each Mutators.all/0 member a corpus of head-matching-but-degenerate nodes through mutate/1 and asserts a clean transform OR a structured skip — never a raise. Better: a seeded-AST property (generate valid Elixir source -> quote -> enumerate/1) asserting enumerate never raises. Keep the project's zero-dep policy (hand-rolled :rand, not StreamData).

Done: mutate/1 proven raise-free on degenerate-but-head-matching AST across all mutators; enumerate/1-never-raises property green.
title: H2: mutate/1 malformed-AST resilience (never raise during enumeration)
